title: lamium moschatum subsp. moschatum
intro: Lamium moschatum subsp. moschatum, also known as Spotted Dead Nettle, is a perennial herb in the mint family native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lamium moschatum subsp. moschatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive problems, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of Lamium moschatum subsp. moschatum include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health issues.
